tool_input.summary: No Arabia Terra–specific (region-named) datasets were returned by the catalog search. To evaluate whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single vs. diverse fluvial/depositional styles, the most directly useful PDS holdings found are global Mars orbital datasets providing (1) mineralogy/spectral constraints (MRO/CRISM), (2) topography for basin/channel geometry (MGS/MOLA), (3) regional image/DTM context (Mars Express/HRSC), and (4) shallow/deeper subsurface layering (MRO/SHARAD; Mars Express/MARSIS). Imaging-node (HiRISE/CTX) dataset IDs were not present in the returned catalog results, so they are not listed here.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected targeted CRISM reduced data (MTRDR) supports facies/mineralogical discrimination (e.g., phyllosilicates/sulfates) to test whether deposits reflect one fluvial style or multiple depositional environments.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CRISM multispectral map products provide broader regional compositional context across Arabia Terra for correlating distinct depositional units.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM VRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CRISM VNIR hyperspectral reduced data enable detailed spectral characterization of altered sediments that can differentiate lacustrine/deltaic vs. alluvial/eolian overprints.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: MOLA gridded topography (MEGDR) is foundational for mapping paleoslopes, basin geometry, channel long profiles, and depositional surface gradients relevant to distinguishing fluvial styles (e.g., distributive vs. confined systems).

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-3-PEDR-ASCII-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PEDR shot-point profiles support detailed elevation checks/cross-sections across candidate depositional landforms in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC regional imaging and stereo-derived products are useful for geomorphic mapping of channel patterns, fan/delta morphologies, and stratigraphic relationships across Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle
candidates.reasoning: SHARAD radargrams can reveal subsurface layering/reflectors that help test whether deposits formed through repeated episodes and varying depositional systems rather than a single fluvial style.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD 3D Radargram Bundle
candidates.reasoning: 3D-focused SHARAD products enable spatially coherent interpretation of buried channels/strata where available, strengthening depositional-system comparisons.

candidates.title: Optimized MARSIS Radargram Data
candidates.reasoning: MARSIS deeper-penetrating radar complements SHARAD for broader-scale subsurface architecture that can indicate basin fill history and system diversity.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?

parts.args: {"summary":"No Arabia Terra–specific (region-named) datasets were returned by the catalog search. To evaluate whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single vs. diverse fluvial/depositional styles, the most directly useful PDS holdings found are global Mars orbital datasets providing (1) mineralogy/spectral constraints (MRO/CRISM), (2) topography for basin/channel geometry (MGS/MOLA), (3) regional image/DTM context (Mars Express/HRSC), and (4) shallow/deeper subsurface layering (MRO/SHARAD; Mars Express/MARSIS). Imaging-node (HiRISE/CTX) dataset IDs were not present in the returned catalog results, so they are not listed
